source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two thin rods are fastened to the inside of a circular ring as shown in figure. One rod is vertical, and the other makes an angle with the horizontal. The two rods and the ring lie in a vertical plane. Two small beads are free to slide without friction along the rods. Find an expression for the time interval required for the blue bead to slide from point \textcircled{B} to point \textcircled{C} in terms of \( g \), \( L \), and \( \theta \).

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
rod orientation, labeled points B and C, and the role of the blue bead are diagram-dependent
registry_passed=true and image_manifest shows 1 existing image
multi_step: solving likely requires combining geometry/diagram interpretation with mechanics of motion along a rod
the answer is an expression in g, L, and theta rather than a direct lookup
the problem appears to require identifying the relevant path length/acceleration and then applying a time relation
multiple-choice options differ by trigonometric factors, indicating some derivation rather than a single factual recall
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific symbolic expression
choices provide a closed set of candidate formulas, making correctness easy to check
the target quantity is a single time-interval expression that can be algebraically verified

decision_reasons: question, answer, and required image asset are all present, so the core semantics are preserved
registry_passed=true with no recorded asset issues, indicating strong asset completeness
high image_dependency_score is adequately supported because the diagram exists in the image manifest
high verifiability_score and a specific short-text symbolic answer make the sample suitable for downstream validation
although some diagram details are not fully specified in text, the available figure mitigates that risk rather than causing a core semantic gap
